items.title: Dolphins to release Tua, take on record cap hit
items.summary: The Dolphins will release quarterback Tua Tagovailoa after the start of the new league year in a move that will leave Miami with an NFL-record $99 million in dead money.

items.title: Sources: Packers trading DL Gary to Cowboys
items.summary: The Green Bay Packers are finalizing a deal that would send defensive lineman Rashan Gary to the Dallas Cowboys in exchange for a 2027 draft pick, sources told ESPN's Adam Schefter. The pick is a fourth-rounder, according to NFL Network.

items.title: Sources: NFL labor talks with refs hit rough patch
items.summary: The NFL's negotiations with the NFL Referees Association on a new collective bargaining agreement are not in a good place, sources told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

items.title: Sources: Texans to bring back Ingram, Rankins
items.summary: Guard Ed Ingram and defensive tackle Sheldon Rankins will not hit free agency after agreeing to multiyear deals with the Texans, according to ESPN and NFL Network.

items.title: Jets acquiring Minkah Fitzpatrick from Dolphins and signing him to $40M extension, AP source says
items.summary: The New York Jets are acquiring five-time Pro Bowl safety Minkah Fitzpatrick from the Miami Dolphins and signing him to a three-year, $40 million contract extension, a person with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press on Monday.  The Jets will send a seventh-round pick in this year's draft to the Dolphins in the trade, according to two people who spoke to the AP on condition of anonymity because the trade can’t become official until the start of the NFL’s new league year on Wednesday.  The deal, first reported by ESPN, addresses a major need for the Jets, who became the first team in NFL history to go an entire season without an interception.
